title: Vying For Attention

jokes.text: Having breakfast with Rico Suave?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is wearing a sombrero while cooking
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to the 90s Latin heartthrob singer
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific observation from Will, economical delivery, topical reference that works

jokes.text: Don't worry, G. I'll have it back in your room in an hour, man.
jokes.setup: Will borrowed Geoffrey's hat without asking
jokes.punchline: Casually promises to return it as if it makes the theft okay
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's casual attitude toward borrowing things, good character consistency

jokes.text: How did this Playboy get in here? / I bought it.
jokes.setup: Vivian finds Playboy in care package she prepared
jokes.punchline: Will casually admits he put it there
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure, economical delivery

jokes.text: It's a special legal edition. The centerfold wants to be a Supreme Court Justice.
jokes.setup: Justifying buying Playboy magazine
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation mixing legal profession with centerfold
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd justification that feels like Will's quick thinking

jokes.text: I've never known anyone in construction. Except for that guy in the Village People.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to relate to meeting a construction worker
jokes.punchline: His only reference is the Village People construction worker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton naivety and sheltered perspective

jokes.text: Yeah, well, his daughter can sleep in my room, but if she touches my stuff... I'm stuffing her in the clothes hamper.
jokes.setup: Ashley being territorial about sharing her room
jokes.punchline: Threatens to stuff a child in hamper
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley's bratty side with dark humor

jokes.text: What? It's not like Hillary never did it.
jokes.setup: Ashley justifying stuffing someone in hamper
jokes.punchline: Implies Hillary has been stuffed in hamper before
jokes.explanation: Good callback to family dynamics, economical delivery

jokes.text: All she's trying to do is get to her retirement plan and her rocking chair. / Is that why she's moonwalking up the driveway?
jokes.setup: Will claims his mom just wants to retire quietly
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out she's literally moonwalking with excitement
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual contradiction of Will's words, great setup/payoff

jokes.text: Divas!
jokes.setup: Vivian dramatically enters moonwalking
jokes.punchline: Will's exasperated one-word reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy, captures Will's attitude in one word

jokes.text: Hey, your complexion has cleared up. / So did yours.
jokes.setup: Will comments on his mom's appearance
jokes.punchline: Mom fires back with same observation
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback humor, shows their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: You're funny. You must be Carlton. / Well, no, you see, actually, Carlton is the funny-looking one over there.
jokes.setup: Robert assumes Will is Carlton based on humor
jokes.punchline: Will clarifies Carlton is the 'funny-looking' one
jokes.explanation: Good setup/correction structure, shows Will's quick wit

jokes.text: See, I'm Will. But you can just call me Your Majesty.
jokes.setup: Will introducing himself formally
jokes.punchline: Suggests royal title instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Will cockiness, good character consistency

jokes.text: I may be small, but I'm not ignorant.
jokes.setup: Will's over-the-top introduction
jokes.punchline: Robert's dry, understated response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan comeback, establishes Robert's character

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Isn't she the cutest little thing you've ever seen? / Not really.
jokes.setup: Hillary gushing over how cute Claudia is
jokes.punchline: Claudia's blunt, deadpan disagreement
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery from child character, subverts expectation

jokes.text: And isn't he the biggest hunk you've ever seen? / Not really.
jokes.setup: Hillary gushing again, this time about Robert
jokes.punchline: Claudia repeats exact same deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Good callback that establishes Claudia's deadpan character trait

jokes.text: First thing she does when she gets anywhere is... she kicks off her shoes... rolls her hair up into one of them twist joints... and goes and puts on one of my big old sweat suits.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil expecting Vivian to freshen up
jokes.punchline: Will describes his mom's unglamorous routine
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific observation, sets up visual contradiction

jokes.text: Sorry, everybody, I just had to freshen up a little bit. You like my new pearls, Will?
jokes.setup: Will's description of mom's usual unglamorous routine
jokes.punchline: Vivian enters dressed up with pearls, complete opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual contradiction, good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Yeah, but they would probably look better with one of my big old sweat suits.
jokes.setup: Vivian showing off her pearls
jokes.punchline: Will references his earlier description, calling out the change
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will noticing the contradiction

jokes.text: Thank you, baby, but I'm eating lighter these days. / You want half my melon?
jokes.setup: Vivian declining Will's heavy breakfast for lighter eating
jokes.punchline: Robert offers half his melon, showing he gets her
jokes.explanation: Shows Robert understanding Vivian's needs, mild relationship humor

jokes.text: I feel a little thirsty, too. / Robert? Do you mind getting Vivian something to drink while you're up there? And toss me a Pudding Pop.
jokes.setup: Philip saying he's thirsty too
jokes.punchline: Then casually asks Robert to serve him too, adding Pudding Pop
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil move, taking advantage of the situation

jokes.text: Oh, sure. It's just gonna break again in 20 years.
jokes.setup: Robert quickly fixes refrigerator
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about the quality
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Will's competitiveness

jokes.text: Tell them, baby. / I'm in construction.
jokes.setup: Vivian excitedly asking Robert to tell about his work
jokes.punchline: Robert's extremely understated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery, character consistency

jokes.text: Yo, I'm working on a big deal. You know, down at the restaurant where I work? I'm about to be promoted... to vice president in charge of keeping bums out of the entryway.
jokes.setup: Will trying to compete with Robert's success
jokes.punchline: Reveals his 'big deal' is basically being a bouncer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and deflation, shows Will's insecurity and bravado

jokes.text: So, Robert, you know how to fix a broken dish? / Sure. You got any superglue? / I was talking about our satellite dish. / So am I.
jokes.setup: Hillary asking about fixing broken dish, Robert assumes dinnerware
jokes.punchline: Reveals satellite dish, Robert maintains he meant that too
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with good recovery from Robert

jokes.text: Well, why? It's hot, it's smoggy... and plus, I won't be able to hear nothing y'all are saying.
jokes.setup: Hillary suggesting Will show Claudia the backyard
jokes.punchline: Will lists reasons why it's bad, including not hearing their conversation
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's suspicion and desire to eavesdrop, good character work

jokes.text: Ashley, honey, where are your manners? / I'm sorry. Did he pop the question, please?
jokes.setup: Vivian correcting Ashley's manners
jokes.punchline: Ashley adds 'please' to the same inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic, excellent character consistency for Ashley

jokes.text: Let the maids worry about the sinks. What does he drive?
jokes.setup: Discussion of his-and-hers sinks in dream house
jokes.punchline: Hillary prioritizes car over house features
jokes.explanation: Good Hillary character moment showing her materialistic priorities

jokes.text: The important question is, does his mother like you? / She's dead. / Girlfriend, you in.
jokes.setup: Asking if Robert's mother likes Vivian
jokes.punchline: Learning she's dead is seen as an advantage
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: I'm not down with O.P.P. anyway.
jokes.setup: Claudia declining to take bracelet from another person
jokes.punchline: References Naughty by Nature song about other people's property
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference from a child character, perfect economy

jokes.text: Claudia, now, after this barbecue... you know, you guys had the junk and all the stuff in your teeth. Did my mom have to go home to use her toothbrush... or did she already have one there?
jokes.setup: Will trying to subtly find out how serious his mom's relationship is
jokes.punchline: Awkward specific question about toothbrush logistics
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Will's desperation for information

jokes.text: Are you trying to trick me to find out how serious they are? / So, what, you think they're pretty serious?
jokes.setup: Claudia calling out Will's obvious scheme
jokes.punchline: Will immediately abandons subtlety and asks directly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will caught red-handed

jokes.text: My brother, it's time you wake up and smell the Play-Doh.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to give Will serious advice
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'coffee' idiom with 'Play-Doh'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton malapropism, great character consistency

jokes.text: Mom! You half-naked! / Come on, you don't want Robert to get the wrong idea. / You damn straight! You don't think I'm doing these butt-busters for my health?
jokes.setup: Will embarrassed by mom's workout outfit
jokes.punchline: Vivian admits she's working out to look good for Robert
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics humor with honest character moment

jokes.text: Okay, that's it, young lady. You go to your room... in Philly.
jokes.setup: Standard parental punishment threat
jokes.punchline: Escalates to sending Will back to Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that references show's premise

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Hold up, Mom. How you gonna play me? Why you ain't hook me up with one of them pink jumpies? / I couldn't find one with a fly.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about not getting workout outfit
jokes.punchline: Mom explains she couldn't find one with a fly (men's feature)
jokes.explanation: Good practical joke with character-specific delivery

jokes.text: Man, Jane Fonda got a butt like Arsenio.
jokes.setup: Will watching workout video
jokes.punchline: Compares Jane Fonda's figure to Arsenio Hall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected comparison, perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: Maybe not. I'd like to have kids one day.
jokes.setup: Will attempting workout moves
jokes.punchline: Realizes he might hurt himself reproductively
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reaction with relatable concern

jokes.text: When you say 'surprise,' you don't mean like... 'I'm driving down a big hill, and all of a sudden: Surprise! My brakes don't work'?
jokes.setup: Robert mentions surprising Will
jokes.punchline: Will's paranoid scenario about brake failure
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid Will moment with creative scenario

jokes.text: It was an oyster bar. You see, I was a little shy, so... Luckily, she came up and she talked to me.
jokes.setup: Robert admits Vivian picked him up in a bar
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it was an oyster bar and he was shy
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection and character development

jokes.text: Is it true you took your diaper off and ran around Sears naked? / You're damn right, and I'd do it again if I had to.
jokes.setup: Robert asking about embarrassing childhood story
jokes.punchline: Will proudly owns the story
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's confidence and defiance

jokes.text: So what kind of marble do you want for those his-and-hers sinks?
jokes.setup: Robert gets the government contract
jokes.punchline: Vivian immediately brings up the sinks from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Vivian's immediate practical thinking

jokes.text: I'd like to impart some words that I've always lived by: Mo' money, mo' money, mo' money.
jokes.setup: Philip offering wisdom about Robert's success
jokes.punchline: His wisdom is just 'more money' repeated
jokes.explanation: Good Uncle Phil moment showing his materialistic wisdom

jokes.text: I have to admit, success is sweet. But having three beautiful women to share it with makes it even sweeter. / Especially when they're triplets.
jokes.setup: Robert's romantic comment about three beautiful women
jokes.punchline: Carlton misinterprets as being about triplets
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton misunderstanding showing his naivety

jokes.text: It's nice to hear you gonna have some money now. I guess them old girlfriends gonna be coming out of the woodworks, huh?
jokes.setup: Will congratulating Robert on his success
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests this will attract gold diggers
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's cynical worldview and jealousy

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, no jumping over the net.
jokes.setup: Philip and Robert going to play tennis
jokes.punchline: Will warns about Philip's size and athleticism
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reference to Uncle Phil's size

jokes.text: Honey, save the rice. You'll be needing it for the wedding.
jokes.setup: Vivian planning special dinner for Robert
jokes.punchline: Hillary assumes wedding is imminent
jokes.explanation: Good practical Hillary observation

jokes.text: I was standing behind Robert in the mirror... Dude ain't got no reflection!
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain his suspicion of Robert
jokes.punchline: Claims Robert is a vampire with no reflection
jokes.explanation: Creative paranoid Will moment with supernatural twist

jokes.text: How about reservations?
jokes.setup: Vivian asking what to cook for special dinner
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests making restaurant reservations instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan with great economy

jokes.text: I feel like something exotic. / Okay. How about Lebanese? / You think Robert will mind swinging by Beirut?
jokes.setup: Will wanting exotic food, Carlton suggests Lebanese
jokes.punchline: Will escalates to actually going to Lebanon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's dramatic thinking

jokes.text: I'm afraid it's drier than happy hour at the Betty Ford Center, madam.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey describing how dry the chicken is
jokes.punchline: Compares to rehab center happy hour (no alcohol)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Geoffrey observation with perfect dark humor and originality

jokes.text: Carlton, you just have to know how to ask. / Now can I have Barbie's head back?
jokes.setup: Claudia explaining how to ask for things properly
jokes.punchline: Carlton's formal request for Barbie's head
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's politeness

jokes.text: I haven't seen him this upset since someone told him he looked like me.
jokes.setup: Carlton commenting on Will being upset
jokes.punchline: References being told he looks like Carlton as ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about the characters' relationship

jokes.text: I thought I was the sweet one. / You said you were the sweet one. When we tried to tell you you weren't... you wouldn't listen because you're too hard-headed.
jokes.setup: Vivian defending herself as the sweet sister
jokes.punchline: Aunt Vivian proves the hard-headed point by her reaction
jokes.explanation: Good circular logic showing the character trait in action

jokes.text: Vivian, I can't get this out of my mind. How can you say that I am hard-headed? / Gee, Vy, I don't know where I could have gotten that idea.
jokes.setup: Vivian still arguing about being called hard-headed
jokes.punchline: Aunt Vivian's sarcastic response proving the point
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the character trait

jokes.text: I see your frightened inner child and I'm here to say... come out and play.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to help Will emotionally
jokes.punchline: Overly therapeutic language ending in playground invitation
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton trying-to-be-deep moment with childish end

jokes.text: You can never watch too much Oprah. If it wasn't for her show, I'd still be in denial about my drinking.
jokes.setup: Will saying Carlton watches too much Oprah
jokes.punchline: Carlton credits Oprah for helping with drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Funny Carlton revelation that seems both absurd and believable

jokes.text: Of course, none of that will help you in the corporate world, but... for the next year or so you're sitting pretty.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing Will's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Undermines them as temporary advantages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton backhanded compliment

jokes.text: if your mom abandons you and gets married... you'll always have me. Because... well, you're like the brother I've always wanted.
jokes.setup: Carlton offering comfort about possible abandonment
jokes.punchline: Sincere declaration of brotherly love
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that balances comedy with heart

jokes.text: For not hugging you, right? / No, you know, for saying that I'm like the brother that you always wanted. And for not hugging me, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton assumes Will thanks him for not hugging
jokes.punchline: Will appreciates both the sentiment and lack of physical contact
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Boy, time flies when you're running up to your bedroom.
jokes.setup: Will asked where he's been
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response about literally running to room
jokes.explanation: Good Will sarcasm showing his attitude

jokes.text: Don't you use that tone of walk with me.
jokes.setup: Will being sarcastic with his mother
jokes.punchline: Vivian mixes up 'tone of voice' with 'walk'
jokes.explanation: Great malapropism that still makes sense in context

jokes.text: All right, but I'm picking my own chair. / Boy, sit down. / What a coincidence. That's the one I was going to pick.
jokes.setup: Will defiantly picking his own chair
jokes.punchline: Claims coincidence when forced to sit
jokes.explanation: Good parent-child dynamic with Will trying to save face

jokes.text: Do you expect me to be alone forever? / Yeah. I mean, you done good this far.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking if Will expects her to stay single
jokes.punchline: Will's honest yes with backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will logic with genuine emotional truth

jokes.text: The guilt alone will get us both ponies.
jokes.setup: Claudia talking about advantages of parents getting together
jokes.punchline: Specifically mentions guilt manipulation for ponies
jokes.explanation: Great child logic about manipulating divorced parents

jokes.text: Well, no, I'm not into using guilt for my personal gain. / Temper tantrums. That's where the money is.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he doesn't manipulate with guilt
jokes.punchline: Claudia suggests temper tantrums as better strategy
jokes.explanation: Good child wisdom about parent manipulation tactics

jokes.text: When you take me to the parks with you, I can attract more babes than a puppy.
jokes.setup: Claudia explaining her value as a sister
jokes.punchline: Compares herself to puppy for attracting women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child observation about dating strategy

jokes.text: Are you kidding, child? You cry more than Richard Simmons.
jokes.setup: Philip saying he only cries when saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: Aunt Vivian compares him to emotional fitness guru
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference with accurate observation

jokes.text: Thanks for letting me polish your shoes... hang up your clothes, and vacuum your room. It was really fun playing Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Claudia thanking Will for the visit
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was doing servant work like Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting child's work to Geoffrey's role

jokes.text: What? It's not like Hillary never did it.
jokes.setup: Reaction to Claudia playing Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Repeats Ashley's earlier line about Hillary
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates running gag about Hillary

jokes.text: I'd like to impart some words that I've always lived by: Mo' money, mo' money....
jokes.setup: Philip trying to give wisdom again
jokes.punchline: Repeats his money mantra from earlier
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces Uncle Phil's character but less impactful

jokes.text: Trying to be a professional here!
jokes.setup: Philip being interrupted during his repeated catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to complain about the interruption
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke breaking fourth wall, very Fresh Prince style

tmdb_overview: Will dislikes his mother's new boyfriend.
